name: Muralla de Finestres Loop Trail
name_original: Muralla China de Finestres – circular de trail running desde el Puente de Penavera
description: This difficult 9.8-mile jogging trail offers unique views of the Muralla China de Finestres and Canelles reservoir.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Embassament de Canelles offer diverse terrain within a unique natural setting. The region is characterized by the large artificial lake, nestled within the Montsec mountain range, close to the Pyrenees. Dramatic cliffs, such as those found in the Mont-rebei Gorge, and imposing natural rock barriers like the Muralla China de Finestres, define the landscape. The area provides a variety of trails, from paths carved into rock faces to loops around the reservoir, suitable for active pursuits. Best jogging routes around Embassament de Canelles The most popular jogging route is Muralla de Finestres Loop Trail, a 9.8 miles (15.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the natural rock barrier known as the Muralla China de Finestres. Another top favourite among local runners is Mont-rebei Gorge – Congost de Mont-rebei loop from Congost de Mont-rebei, a difficult 5.8 miles (9.4 km) path. This trail navigates through the dramatic Mont-rebei Gorge, featuring paths carved into the rock face. Local runners also love the Finestras Abandoned Village loop from Tozal Roi, a 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through the intriguing abandoned village of Finestras, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Jogging around Embassament de Canelles is defined by the reservoir's emerald-green waters, dramatic gorges, and historical abandoned villages. The network of running routes offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Embassament de Canelles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 180 runners have used komoot to explore Embassament de Canelles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes around Embassament de Canelles?
A: The terrain around Embassament de Canelles is quite varied and often challenging. You'll find paths carved directly into rock faces, especially in areas like the &lt;strong&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge&lt;/strong&gt;, as well as loops around the reservoir. The landscape is characterized by dramatic cliffs, natural rock barriers, and the emerald-green waters of the lake itself. Many routes feature significant elevation changes, making them suitable for runners looking for a more demanding experience.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in the Embassament de Canelles area?
A: While many of the routes around Embassament de Canelles are rated as moderate to difficult, there are options that cater to various fitness levels. The region offers a diverse network of trails, and while there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in our current selection, some sections of longer loops or shorter, less steep paths might be suitable for beginners. It's always recommended to check the route details for elevation gain and distance before heading out.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running near Embassament de Canelles?
A: The region is rich in stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can experience the dramatic cliffs of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/494069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, including its impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3435367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Another striking geological formation is the &lt;strong&gt;Muralla China de Finestres&lt;/strong&gt;, an imposing natural rock barrier. You might also encounter the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finestras Abandoned Village&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the past, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6560733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canelles Dam&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Embassament de Canelles?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Embassament de Canelles are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947641718/muralla-de-finestres-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muralla de Finestres Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinese Wall of Finestres loop from Finestres&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a shorter circular experience around this natural wonder.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Embassament de Canelles?
A: The running trails in Embassament de Canelles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the breathtaking landscapes, the unique geological formations like the Mont-rebei Gorge, and the sense of adventure offered by paths carved into rock faces. The stunning views of the emerald-green reservoir are also a consistent highlight for those exploring the area.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging routes around Embassament de Canelles?
A: The Embassament de Canelles area is generally welcoming to outdoor enthusiasts, and many trails are suitable for dogs. However, given the challenging terrain, including paths carved into rock and suspension bridges in areas like the Mont-rebei Gorge, it's crucial to assess if a specific route is appropriate for your dog's fitness level and temperament. Always keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of other trail users and local wildlife. Check specific route details for any restrictions.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running or walking options near Embassament de Canelles?
A: While many of the dedicated running routes are rated moderate to difficult, the broader Embassament de Canelles area offers paths suitable for families. For a more relaxed experience, consider exploring sections of the Camino Natural de Montfalcó al Congost de Mont-rebei, which leads to a recreational area. When planning a family outing, look for routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances, and always check the route description for suitability.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Embassament de Canelles?
A: Embassament de Canelles is a great destination for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for longer runs. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended to avoid the midday heat. The stunning natural beauty, including the reservoir's emerald waters and dramatic gorges, can be enjoyed in any season, though conditions may vary.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Embassament de Canelles region?
A: There are over 25 dedicated running routes available for exploration around Embassament de Canelles. These routes range in difficulty from moderate to difficult, offering a variety of challenges and scenic experiences for runners of all levels.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails in Embassament de Canelles?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the specific trailhead you choose. Popular starting points for routes like those near the Mont-rebei Gorge or the Muralla China de Finestres often have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the komoot route details for specific parking information related to your chosen running path, as some areas may have limited space or require a fee.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments near the jogging routes?
A: While the Embassament de Canelles area is primarily a natural and wild landscape, there are some amenities available. Depending on your specific route, you might find small establishments in nearby villages or at popular access points. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer runs, as services might not be directly adjacent to all trailheads.

Q: What is the longest running route in the Embassament de Canelles area?
A: One of the longer and more challenging running routes in the area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24317677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-rebei Gorge – Cami Vell de Mont-rebei loop from Aparcament de la Pertusa 1&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult path stretches for approximately 34.7 kilometers (21.6 miles), offering an extensive exploration of the region's dramatic landscapes.
tips: Traditionally known as "Roques de la Vila", this unique geological formation was renamed the Chinese Wall of Finestres to encourage tourism in the area. You can access the foot of it by boat through the Canelles reservoir.
The Finestras leave you speechless. It's one of the most spectacular places. It is worth following the small path to the castle with hermitage.
The abandoned village of Finestras is an awe-inspiring place where nature and man have combined their forces to create an unforgettable destination.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Puente de Penavera. Be aware that the track leading to Puente de Penavera can be unpaved, steep, and have irregular ground with scattered rocks and potholes, especially after rain. Some sources recommend a 4x4 vehicle for access to the starting point.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner trail runners?
faqs.answer: While parts of the initial section might be suitable for jogging on wide dirt and gravel tracks, the overall route is classified as difficult for jogging and a moderate hike. It includes steep sections, rocky climbs, and even a 2-meter metal staircase, making it more suited for experienced trail runners or fast-paced hikers with good fitness and sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning scenery year-round, but consider visiting during spring or autumn for more moderate temperatures. Summer can be very hot, and some sections might be challenging after heavy rain due to irregular ground and potholes on access roads.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Muralla China de Finestres trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region. However, given the challenging terrain, including steep, rocky sections and a metal staircase, consider if it's appropriate for your dog's fitness and agility. Always keep your dog on a leash and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Montsec de l'Estall area. There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trail itself. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially concerning parking or specific activities.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is renowned for its spectacular scenery and unique features. You'll encounter the striking natural geological formation known as the Chinese Wall of Finestres, the evocative Finestras Abandoned Village, and the hermitages of San Marcos and San Vicente. The View of the Finestras Rock Formations from Ermita de San Marcos offers particularly breathtaking panoramic views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. Initially, from Puente de Penavera, you might find wide dirt and gravel tracks. However, as you progress, especially towards the 'wall' and hermitages, the terrain becomes more demanding. Expect steep sections, irregular ground, scattered rocks, and even a 2-meter high metal staircase affixed to the rock face.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this trail, which is approximately 15.8 km (9.8 miles) long, is around 2 hours and 9 minutes for jogging. However, given its 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, many trail runners or fast-paced hikers might take longer, especially if stopping to enjoy the views or navigate technical sections carefully.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with sections of other trails. You'll find it overlaps with parts of the La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 30. Enduro Montsec, Montsec Bikepacking Loop 2 - Montsec d'Estall, and La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 29. Vuelta al Montsec de l'Estall.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The area around Puente de Penavera and Finestres is quite remote. There are no immediate cafes or restaurants directly at the trailhead. It's advisable to bring all necessary food and water for your trip. You might find options in larger towns further afield.

faqs.question: What should I know about the Montsec de l'Estall park regulations?
faqs.answer: Montsec de l'Estall is known for its natural beauty. While there are no specific entrance fees for the trail, general park regulations apply. These typically include staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, carrying out all trash, and being mindful of fire restrictions, especially during dry seasons. Always respect the natural environment.

waypoints.name: View of the Finestras Rock Formations from Ermita de San Marcos
